brand: Star Wars Episode I Pepsi Collection
hook: A set of 24 collectible Pepsi cans released for the Star Wars Episode I movie in 1999, each printed with a hidden word that was part of a secret puzzle.
fate: The promotion ran only during the summer of 1999 to coincide with The Phantom Menace. When the campaign ended, the cans stopped being made. Full sets and unopened cases became collector items.
summary: In 1999 Pepsi released a set of 24 collectible cans to promote Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace. Each can showed a different character from the movie and had a secret word hidden on it. If you collected all 24 cans and put the words together, they spelled out three hidden phrases. Some cans were printed in gold and came with instant-win cash prizes. The promotion ran only during 1999 and complete sets became popular with collectors.
notable_products: 24 character cans in the US set, each showing a different character from The Phantom Menace across Pepsi, Diet Pepsi, and Mountain Dew
Hidden secret words printed on each can that spelled out three phrases when all 24 were collected
Gold Yoda instant-win prize cans that could be redeemed for cash
International versions with different packaging in markets like Germany and Japan
overview_md:
**Star Wars Episode I Pepsi Collection** was a set of collectible soda cans that Pepsi made to promote the movie Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace in 1999.

There were 24 different cans in the US set. Each one showed a different character from the movie. Pepsi, Diet Pepsi, and Mountain Dew all had cans in the collection. What made it more than a regular movie promotion was a puzzle hidden across all the cans. Every can had a small secret word printed on it somewhere. If you collected all 24 and found all the words, they came together to spell out three hidden phrases.

That puzzle turned buying soda into a collecting game. Kids and fans spent the summer looking for cans they did not have yet, trading doubles with friends, and checking stores for new shipments. Some cans were harder to find than others, which made completing the set a real challenge.

On top of the puzzle, Pepsi also made a small number of special gold cans. These were instant-win prize cans that could be worth cash. Finding a gold Yoda can was the biggest prize in the promotion.

The campaign also had versions in other countries. Germany and Japan had their own packaging with some different designs, which created separate sets for collectors in those markets.

The promotion ran only during 1999, timed to the release of The Phantom Menace. Once it ended, the cans stopped being made. People who held onto full sets, unopened cases, or store display boxes found that other collectors wanted them. The cans still show up at conventions and on eBay today.

timeline.text: Pepsi locks in the US promotion tied to The Phantom Menace. The 24-can set and the hidden-word puzzle are finalized. Early store displays roll out ahead of the film.
The Phantom Menace opens in US theaters. TV commercials push collecting all 24 cans and finding the hidden phrases.
Peak collecting period. Fans trade cans, share checklists, and hunt for the rare gold Yoda instant-win prize cans.
The promotion winds down and remaining stock sells through. The limited run ends and no more cans are produced.
